# Nano Drift

> Android development at your own pace — build, deploy, and mirror inside VS Code.

Nano Drift is a free, open-source VS Code extension that collapses the Android
build → deploy → observe loop to seconds. Save a source file and your device
refreshes automatically — no terminals, no manual installs, no context switching.

## What it does

- **Drift Loop**: On every file save, Nano Drift detects the change (chokidar,
  300 ms debounce), triggers a Gradle incremental build, pushes the APK via adb,
  and launches the app — end-to-end in ~3 seconds on warm builds.
- **Live Device Screen**: Mirrors your Android device or emulator inside a VS Code
  Webview using scrcpy. Tap, swipe, and type without leaving the editor.
- **Inline Diagnostics**: Kotlin/Java compiler errors are parsed and surfaced
  directly in the VS Code Problems panel with source links.
- **Device Management**: USB, Wi-Fi (Android 11+ pairing), and headless AVD
  emulator support — all from the Command Palette.
- **Build Variants**: Switch between debug, release, staging, or any custom
  Gradle variant with a single setting.
- **Multi-Root Workspace**: Independent watcher + build cycle per folder.

## Architecture

Nano Drift is a TypeScript monorepo: an `extension` package (VS Code API) and a
`daemon` package (chokidar file watcher, Gradle runner, adb wrapper). They
communicate over a lightweight JSON-RPC socket.
